10.1016/j.jmp.2023.102770
Knowledge structure theory (KST:  Doignon & Falmagne, 1985, 1999; Falmagne et al., 2013; Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ) provides a mathematical framework for assessment of knowledge and advices for further learning of individuals. In dichotomous KST, individuals’ answers to items are only two cases: completely correct or incorrect. The dichotomous KST has been generalized to polytomous items.

Schrepp (1997)  provided an attempt to generalize the main KST concepts to items with more than two response alternatives.  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  provided the mathematical foundation for the generalization of KST to the case of more than two ordered response categories. They generalized KST from the perspectives of the linearly ordered set and the complete lattice respectively.  Bartl and Belohlavek (2011)  studied graded knowledge states from the perspective of complete residuated lattice. Recently,  Heller (2021)  generalized quasi-ordinal knowledge spaces to polytomous items, where each item allows for partially ordered response values forming lattices. The approach initiated by Schrepp and Stefanutti et al. can be integrated into the work of Heller. Stefanutti et al. allow for infinite sets of items  
                         Q 
                       and complete lattice  
                         V 
                      , whereas the approach of Heller confines consideration to finite sets. Their works open the field to a systematic generalization of dichotomous KST concepts to the polytomous items.

The concept of well-graded knowledge structure was introduced and improved by Falmagne and Doignon in the finite case ( Doignon & Falmagne, 1997; Falmagne & Doignon, 1988 ). Motivation for investigating the concept of well-gradedness comes from a stochastic theory developed by  Falmagne and Doignon (1988) . The well-gradedness plays a key role in the designment of efficient algorithms to assess knowledge ( Eppstein et al., 2009 ). The well-gradedness of knowledge structures has been investigated in dichotomous KST. Learning space is a special knowledge space, which satisfies the axioms of learning smoothness and learning consistency ( Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ).  Cosyn and Uzun (2009)  showed that every learning space is equivalent to a well-graded knowledge space.

Based on the works of  Heller (2021)  and  Stefanutti et al. (2020) , the well-gradedness can be extended to polytomous items. In this paper, we propose the concept of well-graded polytomous knowledge structure relying on the  
                         δ 
                      -distance ( Heller, 2021 ) of polytomous knowledge states. Here, the  
                         δ 
                      -distance is different from the canonical distance in dichotomous KST. Then we propose the concept of discriminative polytomous knowledge structure and show that a well-graded polytomous knowledge structure is discriminative. We also amend the concept of atom defined by  Stefanutti et al. (2020) . Furthermore, we verify that the basis of a polytomous knowledge space is formed by the collection of all the atoms. We discuss the necessary and sufficient conditions of polytomous knowledge structures to be well-graded polytomous knowledge spaces. Moreover, we provide an example to illustrate that a well-grade polytomous knowledge space is not necessarily a polytomous closure space. It is effective to use well-graded polytomous knowledge space to assess knowledge and guide further learning.

The rest of paper is organized as follows. Section  2  introduces some relevant concepts in KST and polytomous generalization of KST. Section  3  proposes the concept of discriminative polytomous knowledge structure. We investigate the bases of polytomous knowledge spaces in Section  4 . Well-graded polytomous knowledge structures are discussed in Section  5 . Section  6  summarizes the main results of this paper.

A  knowledge domain  
                      
                         Q 
                       is a set of items. A  knowledge state  is a subset  
                         K 
                       of  
                         Q 
                       which an individual is capable of solving in ideal conditions. A pair  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            K 
                            )

                         K 
                       is a family of subsets of  
                         Q 
                       containing at least  
                         ∅ 
                       and  
                         Q 
                      . Since  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            K 
                            = 
                            Q 
                         
                      , we shall sometimes simply say that  
                         K 
                       is the knowledge structure without ambiguity.

                            K

                            L 
                            ∈ 
                            K

                         K

Stanley (1997)  defined a poset  
                         
                            ( 
                            P 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       to be  graded with rank  
                      
                         n 
                       if every maximal chain of  
                         P 
                       has the same length  
                         n 
                      .  Heller (2021)  noted that a lattice  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is graded with rank  
                         n 
                       if and only if every maximal chain of  
                         V 
                       has the same length  
                         n 
                      . If  
                         V 
                       is a lattice and there exists a positive integer  
                         n 
                       such that every maximal chain has the same length  
                         n 
                      , then  
                         V 
                       is said to be a  graded lattice . For more detailed descriptions on polytomous generalization of KST, refer to  Heller (2021)  and  Stefanutti et al. (2020) .

                         Q 
                       and  
                         V 
                       are finite sets. The top and bottom elements of  
                         V 
                       is denoted by  
                         ⊤ 
                       and  
                         ⊥ 
                      .

Remark 1 
                         When two items  
                               
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  q 
                               
                             belong to the same notion, we shall sometimes say that they are equally informative. In dichotomous KST, let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a knowledge structure, the set of all the items contained in the same states as item  
                               p 
                             is denoted by  
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     ♢ 
                                  
                               
                             and is called a notion, where  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        ♢ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     q 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                     ∣ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            .  Doignon and Falmagne (1985)  defined a surmise relation  
                               ≾ 
                             for a given dichotomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             as follows:  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ≾ 
                                  q 
                                  ⇔ 
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  ⋂ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⇔ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Sometimes the relation  
                               ≾ 
                             is said to be the precedence relation. In this case, items  
                               p 
                             and  
                               q 
                             are equally informative if and only if both  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ≾ 
                                  q 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ≾ 
                                  p 
                               
                             hold. 
                         In polytomous situation,  Heller (2021)  defined a precedence relation  
                               
                                  
                                     ≼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                               
                             for a given polytomous knowledge structure  
                               K

                                        ∗

                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v

                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        w 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  V 
                               
                            . In this case, items  
                               p 
                             and  
                               q

Definition 4 
                         
                            Stefanutti et al., 2020 
                         
                         A polytomous knowledge structure in which each notion is a singleton is called a  discriminative polytomous knowledge structure .

A polytomous knowledge structure in which each notion is a singleton is called a  discriminative polytomous knowledge structure .

Example 1 
                         Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     a 
                                     , 
                                     b 
                                     , 
                                     c 
                                     }

                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     0 
                                     , 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     , 
                                     3 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             with the numerical relation ‘less than or equal’ on  
                               V 
                            . Let  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           = 
                                           
                                              {

                                           ,

                                           233 
                                           , 
                                           333 
                                           
                                              } 
                                           
                                           .

                               K

                               K

Definition 5 
                         
                            Heller, 2021 
                         
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge structure. Joins  
                               ⊔ 
                             on  
                               K 
                             are said to be  defined item-wise  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , whenever  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊔ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             then  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     
                                     ⊔ 
                                     
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                  ∨ 
                                  
                                  L 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             holds for all  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            . 
                         Similarly, meets  
                               ⊓ 
                             on  
                               K 
                             are said to be  defined item-wise  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , whenever  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊓ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             then  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     
                                     ⊓ 
                                     
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                  ∧ 
                                  
                                  L 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             holds for all  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            .

Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge structure. Joins  
                               ⊔ 
                             on  
                               K 
                             are said to be  defined item-wise  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , whenever  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊔ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             then  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     
                                     ⊔ 
                                     
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                  ∨ 
                                  
                                  L 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             holds for all  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            .

Similarly, meets  
                               ⊓ 
                             on  
                               K 
                             are said to be  defined item-wise  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , whenever  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊓ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             then  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     
                                     ⊓ 
                                     
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  
                                  ∧ 
                                  
                                  L 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             holds for all  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            .

Definition 6 
                         
                            Stefanutti et al., 2020 
                         
                         A polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a  polytomous knowledge space  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊔ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             and joins on  
                               K 
                             are defined item-wise. 
                         Similarly, a polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a  polytomous closure space  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊓ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             and meets on  
                               K 
                             are defined item-wise.

A polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a  polytomous knowledge space  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊔ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             and joins on  
                               K 
                             are defined item-wise.

Similarly, a polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a  polytomous closure space  if for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                  ⊓ 
                                  
                                  L 
                               
                             exists in  
                               K 
                             and meets on  
                               K 
                             are defined item-wise.

It is rather reasonable that  
                         K 
                       is required to be  
                         ⊔ 
                      -closed ( 
                         
                            K 
                            
                            ⊔ 
                            
                            L 
                         
                       exists in  
                         K 
                       for all  
                         
                            K 
                            , 
                            L 
                            ∈ 
                            K 
                         
                      ) and joins on  
                         K 
                       are defined item-wise. The empirical interpretation of  
                         ∪ 
                      -closure in the dichotomous KST is: “Two students interacting for a while, will end up to merge their initial knowledge states into a single one which is the union of the two knowledge states”. However, in the polytomous case, something more happens because the two students will end up with knowledge that is new for both. From their interaction new knowledge is generated that is different from the transmission of their knowledge to one another. So, their initial polytomous knowledge states are merged into a single one which is the join of the two polytomous knowledge states.

Let  
                         
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                         
                       be finite non-empty sets, and  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       a lattice. A polytomous knowledge structure  
                         K 
                       is called a  quasi ordinal polytomous (knowledge) space  if  
                         K 
                       is a lattice with meets and component-directed joins defined item-wise ( Heller, 2021 ). Theorem 1 in  Heller (2021)  showed that if  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a linear order then there is a one-to-one correspondence between the precedence relations for  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      , and the polytomous knowledge structures  
                         K 
                       on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       that form lattices, where all joins and meets are defined item-wise. Therefore, if  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a linear order, then we have the sequence of inclusions: quasi ordinal polytomous (knowledge) spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       polytomous knowledge spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       polytomous knowledge structures.

This coincides with the dichotomous case, where we have the sequence of inclusions: quasi ordinal knowledge spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       knowledge spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       knowledge structures ( Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ).

Moreover, if  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a lattice, Theorem 2 and 3 in  Heller (2021)  showed that there is a one-to-one correspondence between the precedence relations for  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       on  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      , and the polytomous knowledge structures  
                         K 
                       on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       that form lattices, where all meets and component-directed joins are defined item-wise.  Wang et al. (2023)  proposed the concept of CD-polytomous knowledge space: Let  
                         K 
                       be a polytomous knowledge structure on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                      , then  
                         K 
                       is called a  CD-polytomous knowledge space  if for any component-directed collection  
                         
                            F 
                            ⊆ 
                            K 
                         
                      , the join  
                         
                            ⊔ 
                            F 
                         
                       exists in  
                         K 
                       and is defined item-wise. Obviously, if  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a lattice, then we have the sequence of inclusions: quasi ordinal polytomous (knowledge) spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       CD-polytomous knowledge spaces  
                         ⊆ 
                       polytomous knowledge structures.

Example 2 
                         Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     a 
                                     , 
                                     b 
                                     , 
                                     c 
                                     }

                                  V

                                     0 
                                     , 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     , 
                                     3 
                                     }

                                  0 
                                  ≤ 
                                  1 
                                  ≤ 
                                  3 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  0 
                                  ≤ 
                                  2 
                                  ≤ 
                                  3 
                               
                            . Consider the polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                           = 
                                           
                                              { 
                                           
                                           000 
                                           , 
                                           100 
                                           , 
                                           011 
                                           ,

                                           , 
                                           210 
                                           , 
                                           211 
                                           , 
                                           122 
                                           , 
                                           300 
                                           , 
                                           310 
                                           , 
                                           301 
                                           , 
                                           311 
                                           , 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                           133 
                                           , 
                                           332 
                                           , 
                                           323 
                                           , 
                                           333 
                                           
                                              } 
                                           
                                           . 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            It is trivial to verify that  
                               K 
                             is a polytomous knowledge space.

Let  
                               
                                  Q 
                                  =

                                           , 
                                           332

                                           323

                            It is trivial to verify that  
                               K

For any polytomous knowledge space  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                      , there exists a subfamily of  
                         K 
                       such that  
                         K 
                       can be represented as the spans of the subfamily. The span of a family of polytomous knowledge states can reference to Definition 8 in  Stefanutti et al. (2020) . The subfamily can fully characterize  
                         K 
                       without losing valuable information.

Remark 2 
                         
                            Stefanutti et al. (2020)  proposed the concept of  
                               v 
                            -atom at  
                               p 
                            : Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge structure and  
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     + 
                                  
                               
                             the set of all irreducible levels of  
                               V 
                            . For any  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            , a state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             is a “ 
                               v 
                            -atom” at  
                               p 
                             if the following three conditions are all satisfied: 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (i) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 V 
                                              
                                              
                                                 + 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (ii) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (iii) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ⊑ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                      and  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      imply  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           = 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     , for all  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     . 
                               
                            
                         
                         Then  
                               K 
                             is said to be an “atom” at  
                               p 
                             if it is a  
                               v 
                            -atom at  
                               p 
                             for some  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . 
                         Corollary 1 in  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  illustrated that the basis of a polytomous structure is the collection of all of its “atoms”.  Example 2  in our paper shows that this result is not rigorous. In fact, the family of all “atoms” (by the Definition 5 in  Stefanutti et al., 2020 ) is  
                               
                                  { 
                                  100 
                                  , 
                                  011 
                                  , 
                                  210 
                                  , 
                                  122 
                                  , 
                                  301 
                                  } 
                               
                            . However, consider the polytomous knowledge state 300, there exists no subfamily  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     100 
                                     , 
                                     011 
                                     , 
                                     210 
                                     , 
                                     122 
                                     , 
                                     301 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  300 
                                  = 
                                  ⨆ 
                                  G 
                               
                            . In order to resolve this impasse, we redefine atom as follows.

Stefanutti et al. (2020)  proposed the concept of  
                               v 
                            -atom at  
                               p 
                            : Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge structure and  
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     + 
                                  
                               
                             the set of all irreducible levels of  
                               V 
                            . For any  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            , a state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             is a “ 
                               v 
                            -atom” at  
                               p 
                             if the following three conditions are all satisfied:

(i) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 V 
                                              
                                              
                                                 + 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (ii) 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     ; 
                               
                               
                                  (iii)

                                                 ′

                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈

                                     .

v 
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 V 
                                              
                                              
                                                 + 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     ;

p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈

K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′

                                      and  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      imply  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           = 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     , for all  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 K 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           K 
                                        
                                     .

Then  
                               K 
                             is said to be an “atom” at  
                               p 
                             if it is a  
                               v 
                            -atom at  
                               p 
                             for some  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            .

Corollary 1 in  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  illustrated that the basis of a polytomous structure is the collection of all of its “atoms”.  Example 2  in our paper shows that this result is not rigorous. In fact, the family of all “atoms” (by the Definition 5 in  Stefanutti et al., 2020 ) is  
                               
                                  { 
                                  100 
                                  , 
                                  011 
                                  , 
                                  210 
                                  , 
                                  122 
                                  , 
                                  301 
                                  } 
                               
                            . However, consider the polytomous knowledge state 300, there exists no subfamily  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     100 
                                     , 
                                     011 
                                     , 
                                     210 
                                     , 
                                     122 
                                     , 
                                     301 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  300 
                                  = 
                                  ⨆ 
                                  G 
                               
                            . In order to resolve this impasse, we redefine atom as follows.

Definition 9 
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge space. A polytomous knowledge state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is said to be an atom at  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             if there exists no subfamily  
                               
                                  A 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     K 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  ⨆ 
                                  A 
                               
                            .

Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a polytomous knowledge space. A polytomous knowledge state  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is said to be an atom at  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K

                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        M

                                  ⨆
