For any item  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            v 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                            ∖ 
                            
                               { 
                               ⊥ 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      , the set of all the atoms at  
                         
                            
                               p 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                         
                       is denoted by  
                         
                            
                               σ 
                            
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      .  Example 3  below shows that  
                         
                            
                               σ 
                            
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                       may be empty for some  
                         
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . Moreover, the set of all the atoms of  
                         p 
                       is denoted by  
                         
                            
                               σ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                      . It is obvious that,  
                         
                            
                               
                                  σ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  V 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     ⊥ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  σ 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      . It is an easy observation that  
                         
                            
                               σ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       is nonempty for any  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                      , since  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            K 
                            = 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       (If  
                         
                            
                               
                                  σ 
                               
                               
                                  p 
                               
                            
                            = 
                            ∅ 
                         
                      , then  
                         
                            
                               
                                  σ 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            = 
                            ∅ 
                         
                       for every  
                         
                            v 
                            ∈ 
                            V 
                            ∖ 
                            
                               { 
                               ⊥ 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      , which contradicts  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            K 
                            = 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . So,  
                         
                            
                               σ 
                            
                            
                               p 
                            
                         
                       is nonempty for any  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                      ).  Heller (2021)  showed that for any polytomous knowledge structure  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                      , if  
                         K 
                       is a lattice where the component-directed joins and the meets are defined item-wise, then  
                         
                            ⊓ 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                       is the unique atom at  
                         
                            
                               p 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                         
                      , denoted by  
                         
                            
                               
                                  A 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            = 
                            ⊓ 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      . Besides, each polytomous state  
                         
                            K 
                            ∈ 
                            K 
                         
                       can be obtained as the join of some atoms, i.e.,  
                         
                            K 
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  ⨆ 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  A 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      . In fact, the definition of atom at  
                         
                            
                               p 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                         
                       in the present article coincides with that of  Heller (2021)  whenever  
                         K 
                       is a lattice with joins and meets defined item-wise.

Theorem 1 
                         
                            Every polytomous knowledge space  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            has a basis  
                            
                               
                                  B 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

Every polytomous knowledge space  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            has a basis  
                            
                               
                                  B 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

Proof 
                         Note that  
                               V 
                             and  
                               Q 
                             are finite, then the basis  
                               B 
                             of  
                               K 
                             exists. If there exists  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  B 
                               
                             but  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∉ 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Then for any  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∉ 
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . It follows that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∉ 
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                             for any  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  V 
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     ⊥ 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Then we can choose  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    v 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    p 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    v 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    p 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊏ 
                                  K 
                               
                            . Thus,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⊔ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    v 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    p 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            , contradicting  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  B 
                               
                            . Hence,  
                               
                                  B 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Conversely, take any  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                        ∈ 
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        σ 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Then  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  ⊔ 
                                  F 
                               
                             for some  
                               
                                  F 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  B 
                               
                            . By the definition of atom,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  F 
                               
                            . Hence,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  B 
                               
                            . □

The concepts of learning paths and well-graded knowledge structures were introduced by  Falmagne and Doignon (1988)  in finite cases. Learning space is a special knowledge space, which satisfies the axioms of learning smoothness and learning consistency.  Cosyn and Uzun (2009)  showed that the knowledge structures satisfying the two axioms are equivalent to well-graded knowledge spaces. We generalize the well-gradedness to polytomous knowledge structures.

Definition 10 
                         
                            Stanley, 1997 
                         
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a graded lattice. For any  
                               
                                  v 
                                  ∈ 
                                  V 
                               
                            , let  
                               
                                  ρ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     v 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                             be the length of a maximal chain between the bottom element  
                               ⊥ 
                             and  
                               v 
                            . The mapping  
                               
                                  ρ 
                                  : 
                                  V 
                                  → 
                                  N 
                               
                             is said to be a  length (or rank) function  on  
                               V 
                            .

Theorem 2 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a polytomous knowledge structure and  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            is well-graded. Then  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            is discriminative.

Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q

                             is well-graded. Thus,

                             is well-graded polytomous knowledge space. □

Lemma 1

                             for

Lemma 2

                                        j 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           
                                           ⊓ 
                                           
                                           L 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Thus,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∩ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           
                                           ⊓ 
                                           
                                           L 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . □

Remark 4 
                         
                            Lemma 2  is closely related to Proposition 2  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  Heller (2021) . Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  ≤ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a linear order and  
                               K 
                             a non-empty collection of subsets of  
                               
                                  
                                     V 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                            ,  Heller (2021)  showed that  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is a lattice with joins and meets defined item-wise if and only if  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             is closed for union and intersection. Moreover, if  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  ⊑ 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is a lattice with joins and meets defined item-wise, then  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           
                                           ⊓ 
                                           
                                           L 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∩ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           K 
                                           
                                           ⊔ 
                                           
                                           L 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     
                                        L 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             for all  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            .  Lemma 2  shows that the same conclusion follows whenever  
                               K 
                             is a polytomous knowledge structure, but not necessarily a lattice with joins and meets defined item-wise. Therefore,  Lemma 2  generalizes Proposition 2  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             in  Heller (2021)  to apply to polytomous knowledge structures. 
                         If joins (or meets) on  
                               K 
                             are not defined item-wise, the corresponding result in  Lemma 2  is not necessary true. Let

The following  Theorem 5  provides a method to determine whether a polytomous knowledge structure is a well-graded polytomous knowledge space from its basis. Let  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       be a polytomous knowledge space with the basis  
                         B 
                       and  
                         V 
                       be a linear order, if there exists an atom  
                         
                            B 
                            ∈ 
                            B 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            
                               
                                  B 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                            ∖ 
                            
                               { 
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               } 
                            
                         
                       is not the union of some subfamily of  
                         
                            
                               B 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                      , then  
                         K 
                       is not a well-graded polytomous knowledge space.

Theorem 5 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a polytomous knowledge space with the basis  
                            
                               B 
                            
                            . If  
                            
                               V 
                             
                            is endowed with linear order, then the two following statements are equivalent: 
                         
                         
                            (a)  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            is well-graded; 
                         
                         
                            (b) if  
                            
                               
                                  B 
                                  ∈ 
                                  B 
                               
                             
                            is an atom at  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                             
                            for some  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     , 
                                     v 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  V 
                               
                            
                            , then  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        B 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∖ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             
                            is the union of some subfamily of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     B 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

Remark 5 
                         A knowledge structure  
                               K 
                             is called a  learning space  if it satisfies [L1] and [L2], where 
                         [L1]  Learning smoothness . For any two states  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L 
                               
                            , there exists a finite chain of states  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊂ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊂ 
                                  ⋯ 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  
                                     | 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∖ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           − 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     | 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             for  
                               
                                  1 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  p 
                               
                             and so  
                               
                                  
                                     | 
                                     L 
                                     ∖ 
                                     K 
                                     | 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  p 
                               
                            . 
                         [L2]  Learning consistency . If  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             are two states satisfying  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  ⋃ 
                                  K 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     q 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , then  
                               
                                  L 
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     q 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , where  
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∉ 
                                  K 
                               
                            . 
                         In  Theorem 6 , the condition  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             indicates that learning occurs as increasing the mastery of some single item by moving to the next level in the linear order. The condition  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             indicates that knowing more does not prevent learning new items. Conditions  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             provide generalizations of learning smoothness and learning consistency respectively.

A knowledge structure  
                               K 
                             is called a  learning space  if it satisfies [L1] and [L2], where

                                  K

                                  L 
                               
                             such that

                                  ⊂ 
                                  ⋯ 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  =

                                     |

                                  = 
                                  p 
                               
                            .

[L2]  Learning consistency . If  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             are two states satisfying  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L 
                               
                             and

                            .

In  Theorem 6 , the condition  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             indicates that learning occurs as increasing the mastery of some single item by moving to the next level in the linear order. The condition  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i

                                  ( 
                                  i

                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  i 
                                  i 
                                  ) 
                               
                             provide generalizations of learning smoothness and learning consistency respectively.

                             is also well-graded.

Heller (2021)  and  Stefanutti et al. (2020)  generalized quasi-ordinal knowledge spaces to polytomous items. It is reasonable to use polytomous knowledge states to represent the individuals’ partial mastery of items. Well-gradedness has been widely applied to dichotomous KST, which can be implied by the axioms of learning smoothness and learning consistence of learning spaces. In the present article, we focus on well-graded polytomous knowledge structures, which rely on the  
                         δ 
                      -distance of polytomous knowledge states. A well-graded polytomous knowledge structure can be regarded as the generalization of the well-gradedness to polytomous knowledge structures.

Theorem 1  proves that the basis of every polytomous knowledge space is formed by the collection of all the atoms. Here, the definition of atom is not restrict to the irreducible levels, which is different from that of  Stefanutti et al. (2020) . In  Definition 4 , we define a discriminative polytomous knowledge structure in which each notion is a singleton.  Proposition 1  shows that the notion of equally informative items is equal to that of  Stefanutti et al. (2020) .  Theorem 2  shows that every well-graded polytomous knowledge structure is discriminative.  Theorem 3  can be regarded as the generalization of an “antimatroid” ( Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ) to polytomous knowledge structure. However, it only holds for linear order, an example in  Remark 3  shows that the corresponding result is not necessarily hold for a lattice  
                         
                            ( 
                            V 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       that is not linear order.  Theorems 4 and 5  provide methods to determine whether a polytomous knowledge structure is a well-graded polytomous knowledge space. Note that  Theorems 4 and 5  hold for linear order lattices. For any graded lattice  
                         V 
                      ,  Theorem 6  shows a sufficient and necessary condition for a polytomous knowledge structure to be a well-graded polytomous knowledge space.

In  Example 4 , we give two well-graded polytomous knowledge spaces in which  
                         V 
                       is a graded lattice. From  Example 4 , we can observe that a well-graded polytomous knowledge space  
                         K 
                       is not necessary meets on  
                         K 
                       are defined item-wise.

To construct a nonempty collection of knowledge states of a certain population is the core of dichotomous KST. The query ( Heller, 2004; Kambouri et al., 1994; Koppen, 1993 ) and ps-query ( Cosyn & Thiery, 2000 ) algorithms have been designed to build knowledge structures and knowledge states by interviewing experienced experts.  Koppen and Doignon (1990)  establishes a bijective correspondence between knowledge spaces and entail relations.  Eppstein et al. (2009)  proposed a solution to the problem of constructing a learning space through an elaboration of query. It is natural to consider how to construct polytomous knowledge structures and well-graded polytomous knowledge spaces. The query and ps-query algorithms may be valid for constructing well-graded polytomous knowledge structures.

Another effective approach for constructing knowledge structures is from latent cognitive abilities. Skills identifying latent cognitive abilities can be used to interpret individuals’ knowledge states ( Doignon, 1994; Heller et al., 2017, 2013; Stefanutti & de Chiusole, 2017; Sun et al., 2021, 2022 ). In dichotomous cases, the competence-based extension of KST was greatly developed for the assessment of knowledge and guiding further learning ( Anselmi et al., 2017; Heller et al., 2015; Korossy, 1999; Spoto & Stefanutti, 2020 ). In polytomous cases, it may be useful to establish well-graded polytomous knowledge structures from the perspective of latent cognitive abilities.
